restaurants.name: 99 Restaurants
restaurants.address: 11 Taft Corners Shopping Center, Williston, VT 05495, USA

restaurants.name: A Single Pebble
restaurants.attr_evidence: Family-style Chinese banquet house in a converted house — round tables built for groups of 8+; conversational rooms; small bar works solo; shared plates push past $15/head.
restaurants.blurb: Chinese banquet-style dining in a Bank Street rowhouse, built for sharing around a full table. The mock eel — crispy shiitake, no eel involved — converts skeptics nightly.

restaurants.name: Ahli Baba's Kabob Shop
restaurants.attr_evidence: Tiny late-night kabob counter; loud drunken rush after midnight; kabobs/falafel well under $15; no seating for groups.
restaurants.blurb: Main Street's late-night kabob counter — open until 3 AM on weekends, which makes it the last honest meal standing between downtown and tomorrow.

restaurants.name: Al's French Fries
restaurants.address: 1251 Williston Rd, South Burlington, VT 05403, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Counter-service institution with big seating area and some outdoor tables (seed Both); fries/burgers/creemees all cheap; trivially solo-friendly.
restaurants.blurb: Fresh-cut fries from a South Burlington spot running since 1948. The formula hasn't needed revision in 76 years. The soft-serve is mandatory and Vermont-correct.

restaurants.name: American Flatbread (Burlington Hearth)
restaurants.address: 115 St Paul St, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Seasonal patio on St. Paul St (local knowledge); lively hearth-oven room, big tables, very group/family friendly (seed tags); bar pours house Zero Gravity-lineage beers — good solo; flatbreads $18+ meant to share.
restaurants.blurb: Wood-fired flatbread from a blazing hearth you can watch from your table, plus house-brewed beer — this is the room where Zero Gravity brewed before Pine Street existed.

restaurants.name: August First Bakery & Café
restaurants.address: 149 S Champlain St, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Bakery-cafe famous for banning laptops to encourage conversation; outdoor tables in season; seed Solo tag; sandwiches/soups under $15.
restaurants.blurb: The bakery-café that famously banned laptops — the screen-free rule makes it one of the few rooms downtown where lunch is an actual conversation. The bread program is the real draw.

restaurants.name: Beansie's Bus
restaurants.address: 1 Sherman st, Battery Park Rd, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Walk-up bus at Battery Park, all outdoor picnic seating; trivially solo/group friendly; dogs-and-fries pricing far under $15.
restaurants.blurb: A school bus parked at Battery Park slinging burgers, dogs, and fries — a Burlington fixture for generations. Order, then eat on the bluff overlooking the lake.

restaurants.name: Ben & Jerry's (Church Street Scoop Shop)
restaurants.blurb: The downtown scoop shop a few blocks from the gas station on St. Paul Street where Ben and Jerry started in 1978. Mostly symbolic — but the soft-serve is better than expected.

restaurants.name: Black Flannel
restaurants.address: 21 Essex Way #201, Essex Junction, VT 05452, USA

restaurants.name: Bluebird Barbecue
restaurants.attr_evidence: Outdoor Seating seed tag; casual BBQ room suits groups (seed Groups tag); bar seating; sandwiches land under $15 though plates run $14-22.
restaurants.blurb: South End BBQ with long-smoked brisket and house-made sides, in an outdoor picnic-table setup that suits the industrial neighborhood. Get there before the brisket sells out.

restaurants.name: Boxcar Bakery
restaurants.address: 11 Park St Ste 101, Essex Junction, VT 05452, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Cozy Essex Jct neighborhood bakery (seed tags); quiet, cheap, easy solo.
restaurants.blurb: Essex Junction's neighborhood bakery — worth knowing about if you're anywhere near the Five Corners, and worth the detour if you're not.

restaurants.name: Brio Coffeeworks
restaurants.address: 266 Pine St Ste 116, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Roaster-cafe on Pine St; quiet, solo-oriented; coffee + pastry only, no real meals.
restaurants.blurb: The roastery inside the Soda Plant — watch beans roast while you drink them, then wander the building's studios with the cup in hand.

restaurants.name: Bueno Y Sano
restaurants.address: 150 Dorset St Ste 300, South Burlington, VT, 05403

restaurants.name: Burlington Bay Market & Café
restaurants.blurb: A bayside market and café best known for Vermont creemees made with local dairy, with flavors that rotate by the week. The location near Perkins Pier makes it the right last stop before a waterfront walk.

restaurants.name: Burlington Beer Company
restaurants.attr_evidence: Roundups highlight its patio ('patio takes center stage', dog-friendly) despite seed indoor tag; large lively hall appears in group-dining roundups; wood-fired pizza/smash burger menu has sub-$15 items.
restaurants.blurb: A big converted warehouse taproom with 30+ taps and a rotating list that swings between reliable and experimental. Good for a group that can't agree on a style.

restaurants.name: Cafe Dim Sum
restaurants.address: 95 St Paul St, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Low-key ONE spot (seed Laid-back); dim sum sharing suits family groups; cheap eats seed tag — easily under $15.
restaurants.blurb: Handmade dumplings in the Old North End — a small, unfussy room doing dim sum staples that would hold their own in a much bigger city.

restaurants.name: Champ's Legendary Creemees
restaurants.blurb: The creemee window at ECHO on the waterfront, named for the lake monster — maple twist in one hand, sunset over the breakwater in the other.

restaurants.name: Citizen Cider
restaurants.address: 180 Flynn Ave Bldg 2, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Outdoor Seating + dog-friendly seed tags; big communal-table tasting room gets loud; long bar is a classic solo seat; sandwiches/poutine land under $15.
restaurants.blurb: Hard cider from Vermont apples at a Pine Street taproom that does food well enough to justify a full evening. The Wit's Up is the right first pour for anyone who doesn't usually drink cider.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: Local guide (dropt.beer) reports tasting-room deals on flights/light bites; days/times unverified

restaurants.name: City Market
restaurants.address: 82 S Winooski Ave, Burlington, VT 05401, USA

restaurants.name: Cold Hollow Cider Mill
restaurants.address: 3600 Waterbury-Stowe Rd, Waterbury Center, VT 05677, USA
restaurants.blurb: Waterbury Center's cider mill — watch the press run, then eat a cider donut warm from the fryer, which is the only correct way. The mandatory Route 100 stop.

restaurants.name: Cosmic Grind
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open daily ~9am-3/4pm; Yelp updated June 2026. https://thecosmicgrind.com/
restaurants.blurb: Open daily ~9am-3/4pm; Yelp updated June 2026. https://thecosmicgrind.com/

restaurants.name: Deep City
restaurants.attr_evidence: deepcityvt.com: patio with lake/Adirondack views next to Foam; now a Penny Cluse-alum brunch spot — daytime, conversational, event space handles groups; brunch plates hover at/under $15.
restaurants.blurb: The kitchen right next to Foam Brewers — order food here, drink Foam's beer, watch the lake do its evening thing. The system works.

restaurants.name: Deli 126
restaurants.attr_evidence: Intimate speakeasy-style jazz/cocktail bar; conversation works between sets but live music rules the room (quiet_talk low confidence); bar seat solo is the move; small room, cocktail prices.
restaurants.blurb: One part New York deli, one part 1920s jazz lounge — live bands regularly, cocktails that commit to the era, and lighting that flatters everyone.

restaurants.name: Devil Takes a Holiday
cuisine: Cocktail bar with full food menu
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open nightly; kitchen till 11pm-midnight (see late_night).
restaurants.blurb: Open nightly; kitchen till 11pm-midnight (see late_night).

restaurants.name: Doma Bar
cuisine: Cocktail and vinyl listening bar with snacks (shrimp cocktail, pretzels); replaced Paradiso Hi-Fi
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open Tue-Sat evenings, until midnight Fri/Sat.
restaurants.blurb: Open Tue-Sat evenings, until midnight Fri/Sat.

restaurants.name: Donwoori
cuisine: Modern Korean (Korean fried chicken, surf-and-turf fried rice); siblings Summer and Khoi Cao
restaurants.address: 22 Main St, Winooski, VT 05404 (some coverage cites 65 Winooski Falls Way; just east of Rotary Park)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Opened spring 2025 after two years on Williston Rd in South Burlington.
restaurants.blurb: Opened spring 2025 after two years on Williston Rd in South Burlington.

restaurants.name: Dumb Luck Pub & Grill
cuisine: Pub classics (nachos, wings, burgers); second location of the Hinesburg pub, in former Wicked Wings spot
restaurants.attr_evidence: Aimed for Nov 1, 2024 opening; confirmed operating in 2025-2026 (Seven Days Burger Week Nov 2025). Open till 10-11pm nightly.
restaurants.blurb: Aimed for Nov 1, 2024 opening; confirmed operating in 2025-2026 (Seven Days Burger Week Nov 2025). Open till 10-11pm nightly.

restaurants.name: EB Strong's Steakhouse
restaurants.address: 10 Church St, Burlington, VT 05401, Burlington, VT, 05401

restaurants.name: Ecobean Organic Cafe
restaurants.address: 150 Dorset St Suite 304, South Burlington, VT, 05403

restaurants.name: El Gato Essex
restaurants.address: 4 Park St, Essex Junction, VT 05452, USA

restaurants.name: Farmhouse Tap & Grill
restaurants.attr_evidence: Tucked-away beer garden (Seven Days roundup); basement Parlor bar is first-come and a known solo seat; gastropub is packed and loud at dinner (garden calmer); burgers $16-20; famously no-TV beer bar.
restaurants.blurb: Farm-to-table in the most Vermont-specific sense: suppliers are named on the menu and most are within thirty miles. The beef burger is the standard against which Burlington burgers get measured.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: Weekday specials reported in HH roundups (Yelp/happyhopper); exact days/times unverified

restaurants.name: Fiddlehead Brewing Company
restaurants.attr_evidence: Shelburne tasting room with outdoor picnic area (seed Both); no kitchen — Folino's next door (BYOB), so under_15 depends on the pizza next door.
restaurants.blurb: Shelburne's open secret, known best by the people who follow Thursday can releases on social media. The IPA sells out. The taproom is low-key; the beer isn't.

restaurants.name: Foam Brewers
restaurants.attr_evidence: Lakeside taproom with outdoor seating (seed vibe + waterfront roundups); lively music venue vibe, communal tables suit groups; food is small plates only.
restaurants.blurb: Waterfront-adjacent hazy IPAs with a patio that fills up fast on warm evenings. Go on a Tuesday when the wait isn't and the light off the lake is still good. The Vacation IPA is a reliable starting point.

restaurants.name: Folino's Pizza - Burlington
restaurants.address: 71 S Union St #1, Burlington, VT 05401, Burlington, VT, 05401

restaurants.name: Folino's Wood Fired Pizza
restaurants.address: 71 S Union St #1, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Shelburne wood-fired pizza with outdoor picnic seating (local knowledge); casual group/family format; pies $15-20 blur the under-$15 line.
restaurants.blurb: Wood-fired pies next door to Fiddlehead Brewing in Shelburne — the original location is BYOB, so the move is a crowler from the brewery carried thirty feet to your table.

restaurants.name: Four Corners of the Earth
restaurants.attr_evidence: Tiny cash-only sandwich shop on Pine St; quirky, quiet, grab-and-go; sandwiches ~$10-12; no room for a party of 8.
restaurants.blurb: Cash-only sandwiches on Pine Street with combinations you won't see anywhere else — locals argue favorites, but the lamb comes up every time. Bring bills and patience; it's worth both.

restaurants.name: Four Quarters Brewing
restaurants.attr_evidence: Winooski taproom with outdoor seating (seed Both); taproom bar fine solo; food via rotating trucks/pop-ups.
restaurants.blurb: Winooski's taproom anchor — top-shelf beer with a kitchen that takes grilled cheese and burgers as seriously as the brewing. Walk the Riverwalk after.

restaurants.name: Frankie's
cuisine: Dinner restaurant, seafood-leaning New American (GM Cindi Kozak, chef Jordan Ware; opened April 2024)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open; Yelp reviews through Feb 2026. https://www.frankiesvt.com/
restaurants.blurb: Open; Yelp reviews through Feb 2026. https://www.frankiesvt.com/

restaurants.name: Fresh Bubble Tea & Juice
restaurants.address: 155 Dorset St Ste D17, South Burlington, VT, 05403

restaurants.name: Gaku Ramen
restaurants.attr_evidence: Busy Church St ramen bar — counter seating is ideal solo; loud at dinner (seed Lively); bowls ~$14-16 with some under $15; booths max out well below 8.
restaurants.blurb: Proper ramen downtown — rich tonkotsu, a busy room, and exactly what a Vermont February demands at 6 PM.

restaurants.name: Good Times Café
restaurants.address: 1330 Shelburne Rd Suit 2, South Burlington, VT, 05403

restaurants.name: Handy's Lunch
restaurants.attr_evidence: Old-school family diner — counter stools for solo regulars, small room, cheap breakfast; closes early afternoon.
restaurants.blurb: A family-run lunch counter on Maple Street that's fed Burlington for generations — cramped booths, big plates, and regulars who order without looking at the menu.

restaurants.name: Haymaker Buns
cuisine: Bakery/cafe (Haymaker Bun Co., Middlebury's second location; brioche buns, Brio espresso; opened July 2024)
restaurants.address: 266 Pine St, Burlington, VT 05401 (the Soda Plant)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open 7 days; Yelp updated Apr 2026. https://www.haymakerbuns.com/
restaurants.blurb: Open 7 days; Yelp updated Apr 2026. https://www.haymakerbuns.com/

restaurants.name: Hen of the Wood
restaurants.attr_evidence: Dim, intimate fine-dining room built for conversation; bar is walk-in and a beloved solo seat ($1 oyster hour); parties of 8 need advance planning; firmly $$$.
restaurants.blurb: The state's benchmark farm-to-table room — the mushroom toast never leaves the menu because it can't. Waterbury has the original in an old grist mill; Burlington's is the easier reservation.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: $1 oysters at the bar 4-5pm (bar opens at 4); late-night 10-11pm Sun-Thu also reported (FB/Tripadvisor)

restaurants.name: Henry Street Deli
restaurants.attr_evidence: Neighborhood deli counter — cheap sandwiches, grab-and-go, minimal seating.
restaurants.blurb: The Old North End's essential corner deli — sandwiches with a neighborhood following that never needed downtown's foot traffic to thrive.

restaurants.name: Henry's Diner
restaurants.attr_evidence: Classic 1925 diner: counter stools for solo breakfast, booths for easy conversation; cheap; too small for parties of 8.
restaurants.blurb: The quintessential downtown diner, serving Bank Street since the 1920s — booths, big breakfasts, and coffee refills that arrive before you ask.

restaurants.name: Hill Farmstead Brewery
restaurants.address: 403 Hill Rd, Greensboro Bend, VT 05842, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Rural Greensboro destination brewery: outdoor pours with pastoral views, quiet by location; beer pilgrims routinely go solo; no meals; retail-line format not built for seating 8.
restaurants.blurb: Regularly rated the best brewery on the planet, on a Greensboro hilltop an hour-plus from Burlington — a pilgrimage, not an errand, and beer people plan whole trips around it.

restaurants.name: Honey Road
restaurants.attr_evidence: Seasonal outdoor tables at Church/Main cited in outdoor-dining roundups; famously loud, lively room; walk-in bar seating is the local solo move; mezze prices add up well past $15.
restaurants.blurb: Eastern Mediterranean mezze on Church Street — the fried halloumi has a citywide reputation, and the whole menu is built for sharing. Reservations are tough; walk-in bar seats are the workaround.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: 'Honey Time' 4-5pm Mon-Sat: $1 wings + cocktail/snack deals (VT bans drink-price happy hours, hence the name)

restaurants.name: Insomnia Cookies
restaurants.attr_evidence: Seven Days late-night roundup. 84 Church St. https://www.sevendaysvt.com/food-drink/six-spots-for-late-night-food-in-burlington-and-winooski-41549798/

restaurants.name: Jamaican Supreme
restaurants.address: 469 Lime Kiln Rd, South Burlington, VT, 05403

restaurants.name: Jitters Cafe & Lounge
cuisine: Cafe by day, evening lounge with small plates and full bar (2nd location of Jitters from Melrose, MA)
restaurants.address: 130 Bank St, Suite 105 (Burlington Square, aka 142 Bank St corner unit), Burlington, VT 05401
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open daily 7am-7pm.
restaurants.blurb: Open daily 7am-7pm.

restaurants.name: Juniper Restaurant & Bar at Hotel Vermont
Kestrel Coffee
restaurants.address: 47 Maple St, Burlington, VT 05401 (Karma Bird House; downtown cafe)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Company open; but its 77 Pine St cafe closed permanently Apr 17, 2026. Maple St cafe open Mon-Fri 8am-3pm; also BTV airport cafe. https://kestrelcoffees.com/visit/
restaurants.blurb: Company open; but its 77 Pine St cafe closed permanently Apr 17, 2026. Maple St cafe open Mon-Fri 8am-3pm; also BTV airport cafe. https://kestrelcoffees.com/visit/

restaurants.name: Kismet
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open Tue-Sun 12-9pm. Distinct from the defunct Montpelier brunch spot Kismet. https://www.kismetburlington.com/
restaurants.blurb: Open Tue-Sun 12-9pm. Distinct from the defunct Montpelier brunch spot Kismet. https://www.kismetburlington.com/

restaurants.name: Kountry Kart Deli
restaurants.attr_evidence: Late-night deli counter institution; loud rush at bar close; solo grab-and-go by design; subs well under $15; minimal seating.
restaurants.blurb: The downtown grab-and-go institution — fast at noon, open late, and responsible for more UVM morning-after recoveries than any kitchen in town.

restaurants.name: La Di Da Pizza
cuisine: New York-style pizza slice shop, woman-owned (chef Celine Eid), mostly veggie menu
restaurants.address: 294 N Winooski Ave, Ste 112C, Burlington, VT 05401
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open till 10pm Fri/Sat, 9pm other nights, closed Tue.
restaurants.blurb: Open till 10pm Fri/Sat, 9pm other nights, closed Tue.

restaurants.name: Lake Champlain Chocolates (Factory Store)
restaurants.blurb: Vermont's chocolate maker's Pine Street factory store — seconds and overruns at real discounts, plus an ice-cream counter locals treat as a destination on its own.

restaurants.name: Last Stop
restaurants.address: 12 Malletts Bay Ave, Winooski, VT 05404, USA

restaurants.name: Leunig's Bistro & Café
restaurants.attr_evidence: Iconic Parisian-style sidewalk seating on Church St (multiple roundups); white-tablecloth bistro fine for conversation (live jazz some nights); classic bar for solo diners; dinner entrees $24-38.
restaurants.blurb: A French-style bistro on Church Street that's been there long enough to feel like civic infrastructure. Outdoor café seating on the pedestrian mall; the wine list is better than the prices suggest.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: 'Bistro Dinner' 3-5pm: reduced-price entrees + soup/salad + house wine (leunigsbistro.com)

restaurants.name: Lincoln's
restaurants.attr_evidence: Basement dive, cash-only, everything $5 (loveburlington.org); GoodRec lists it among Burlington's best bars for watching soccer (multiple screens); busy and loud; $5 chili mac = meal under $15.
restaurants.blurb: Every drink is five dollars, it's cash only, and the entrance is deliberately hard to find — a basement speakeasy that runs on word of mouth. Now you know.

restaurants.name: Mad River Distillers Tasting Room
restaurants.address: 137 St Paul St, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Small Church St spirits tasting room — quiet, conversational, counter tastings fine solo; no meals; small footprint.
restaurants.blurb: The downtown tasting room for the Warren distillery — Vermont-made whiskey and rum in classic cocktails, a calmer alternative to the beer-first default.

restaurants.name: Majestic
cuisine: Neighborhood cafe and bar; snacks, wine, cocktails, beer; 25 seats, no reservations
restaurants.address: 616 S Willard St, Burlington, VT 05401 (on the South End rotary, former Majestic Car Rental)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Slightly before the 2025 window (Thanksgiving week 2024) but new enough to feature. Weeknights only, opens 4pm.
restaurants.blurb: Slightly before the 2025 window (Thanksgiving week 2024) but new enough to feature. Weeknights only, opens 4pm.

restaurants.name: May Day
restaurants.address: 258 N Winooski Ave, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Cozy neighborhood date spot (seed Cozy/Underrated); bar seating suits solo snacking; small room; snacky plates add up past $15.
restaurants.blurb: An Old North End neighborhood restaurant that cooks like it has downtown rent to justify — small, personal, and easier to book than anything on Church Street.

restaurants.name: Mirabelles Bakery & Café
restaurants.address: 3060 Williston Rd, South Burlington, VT 05403, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Genteel bakery-cafe (seed Cozy + Solo); pastries and lunch under $15; small room.
restaurants.blurb: The pastry case Burlington bakers get judged against — a longtime downtown fixture now on Williston Road, still doing the cakes people order weeks ahead.

restaurants.name: Misery Loves Co.
restaurants.attr_evidence: Yelp/Tripadvisor confirm bar seating and outdoor seating; now a daytime market/restaurant — sandwiches under $15; smallish room for big parties.
restaurants.blurb: A Winooski kitchen that grew from a food truck into one of the state's most inventive rooms — the menu shifts constantly and rewards trust. Brunch here quietly outclasses most dinners in town.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: Reviews cite a happy-hour food menu at good prices; current days/times unverified (now mostly daytime hours, 9am-7pm)

restaurants.name: Morning Light Bakery
cuisine: Hong Kong-style bakery cafe (pastries, cakes, bubble tea)
restaurants.address: 106 E Allen St, Ste 101, Winooski, VT 05404
restaurants.attr_evidence: In Winooski, not Burlington proper. Open Thu-Sun 9am-3pm. https://www.morning-light-vt.com/
restaurants.blurb: In Winooski, not Burlington proper. Open Thu-Sun 9am-3pm. https://www.morning-light-vt.com/

restaurants.name: Ms. Weinerz
restaurants.attr_evidence: Tiny sourdough-donut operation (also stocked at City Market); grab-and-go; donuts aren't a meal — under_15 depends on savory offerings.
restaurants.blurb: Sourdough donuts with a cult that empties the racks by mid-morning — funkier, chewier, and better than the word "donut" prepares you for.

restaurants.name: Muddy Waters
restaurants.attr_evidence: Cave-like, cozy coffeehouse (seed Cozy/Solo); great for lingering conversation; snacks not meals; possible small back deck unverified.
restaurants.blurb: All wood, plants, and low light — a Main Street coffee cave that hasn't changed in decades and would face a riot if it tried. The read-a-book-in-the-corner café.

restaurants.name: Myer's Bagels
restaurants.attr_evidence: Montreal-style bagel bakery with picnic tables outside (local knowledge, low confidence on patio); daytime counter spot, cheap and easy solo.
restaurants.blurb: Montreal-style bagels from a wood-fired oven — smaller, denser, and sweeter than the New York kind, boiled in honey water the way the Mile End shops do it.

restaurants.name: New World Tortilla
restaurants.attr_evidence: Counter-service burrito shop; quick, cheap (~$10-12), minimal seating.
restaurants.blurb: Big grilled wraps on Pine Street that have fed South End lunch breaks for decades — fast, filling, and priced like it's still the year they opened.

restaurants.name: Nomad Coffee
restaurants.address: 208 Flynn Ave, Ste 3i, Burlington, VT 05401 (South End); also 3 Main St, Essex Junction
restaurants.attr_evidence: Burlington location open daily 8am-2pm. https://www.nomadcoffeevt.com
restaurants.blurb: Burlington location open daily 8am-2pm. https://www.nomadcoffeevt.com

restaurants.name: Offbeat Creemee
restaurants.blurb: An all-vegan creemee window in Winooski with flavors that change constantly and never play safe. Coconut-based, and you'd have to be told.

restaurants.name: Onyx Tonics Specialty Coffee
restaurants.attr_evidence: Small specialty-coffee tasting bar; quiet, solo-oriented (seed Solo tag); coffee and pastry only — no real meals.
restaurants.blurb: A coffee bar for people who ask where the beans are from — tasting-menu seriousness applied to espresso, minus the attitude that usually rides along with it.

restaurants.name: Palmer Lane Maple
restaurants.address: 19 Old Pump Rd, Jericho, VT 05465, USA
restaurants.blurb: The maple creemee that ends arguments — a Jericho stand worth the twenty-minute drive, with real Vermont syrup in the mix and a summer-weekend line that moves fast.

restaurants.name: Philo Ridge Farm
restaurants.address: 2766 Mt Philo Rd, Charlotte, VT 05445, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Pastoral farm dining with outdoor terrace (seed Both/Scenic); quiet by design; market-cafe counter makes daytime solo visits easy (low confidence); upscale pricing, reservations recommended.
restaurants.blurb: A working regenerative farm in Charlotte serving meals grown in sight of your table — pair it with Mt. Philo across the road and you've built the ideal Vermont afternoon.

restaurants.name: Pho Hong
restaurants.address: 325 N Winooski Ave, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Family-run Vietnamese standby; casual and calm enough to talk; solo pho at a table is normal; big tables fit families/groups; pho ~$13-15.
restaurants.blurb: The North Street Vietnamese kitchen locals measure all pho against — broth with real depth, a menu that goes far past soup, and prices that haven't noticed the decade.

restaurants.name: Pingala Café
restaurants.attr_evidence: Chace Mill location has riverside outdoor seating (seed Scenic); solo-friendly counter cafe; vegan plates mostly under $15.
restaurants.blurb: All-vegan comfort food in the old Chace Mill by the Winooski River — the breakfast sandwiches convert people who came to scoff.

restaurants.name: Pizzeria Verità
restaurants.address: 156 St Paul St, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Lively Neapolitan room (seed Lively); appears in group-dining roundups; good bar for solo pizza + cocktail; pies $16-22.
restaurants.blurb: Blistered Neapolitan pies from a wood oven and a cocktail list that takes itself as seriously as the dough. The local default answer to "best pizza downtown."

restaurants.name: Poco
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open; active listings on Love Burlington, Hello Burlington, Yelp. https://www.restaurantpoco.com/
restaurants.blurb: Open; active listings on Love Burlington, Hello Burlington, Yelp. https://www.restaurantpoco.com/

restaurants.name: Poor House Pies
restaurants.blurb: Pies from a little stand in Underhill that people describe with genuine warning labels — buy one for the household and it may not survive the drive home.

restaurants.name: Poppy Cafe & Market
restaurants.attr_evidence: Cozy ONE cafe/market (seed Solo + Cheap Eats); easy solo breakfast/lunch under $15; small footprint.
restaurants.blurb: Breakfast and lunch in the Old North End with market shelves along the wall — the kind of café a neighborhood grows around rather than the other way round.

restaurants.name: Prohibition Pig
restaurants.address: 23 S Main St, Waterbury, VT 05676, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Perpetually packed Waterbury BBQ brewpub; bar + adjacent taproom great solo; long waits make parties of 8 dicey; sandwiches ~$14-16 keep under-$15 plausible.
restaurants.blurb: Waterbury barbecue with its own brewery attached — the anchor stop on the I-89 beer corridor, and the smart lunch between the Alchemist and Cold Hollow.

restaurants.name: Queen City Brewery
restaurants.attr_evidence: Low-key European-style taproom (seed Both/Laid-back); quieter than the Pine St giants; food is limited/food-truck dependent.
restaurants.blurb: Old-world lagers and ales brewed exactly to style, across the street from Zero Gravity — the Rauchbier, made with smoked malt, is the one to be brave about. Pizza 44 is attached for deep-dish.

restaurants.name: Queen City Cat Lounge
cuisine: By-the-hour cat lounge/adoption space - NOT primarily a cafe (no notable food/drink service)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open Wed-Thu 10am-6pm (split); $15/hr adults. Categorize as activity, not food/drink. https://www.queencitycatlounge.com/
restaurants.blurb: Open Wed-Thu 10am-6pm (split); $15/hr adults. Categorize as activity, not food/drink. https://www.queencitycatlounge.com/

restaurants.name: Red Hen Baking Co.
restaurants.attr_evidence: Middlesex bakery-cafe with outdoor tables (local knowledge, low confidence on patio); quiet daytime stop; bread/sandwiches under $15.
restaurants.blurb: The Middlesex bakery whose bread stocks half the good restaurants in the state — the café at the source is the mandatory pit stop when driving I-89 south.

restaurants.name: Revolution Kitchen
restaurants.attr_evidence: Small, cozy vegetarian date-night room (seed Cozy + Solo tags); quiet enough to talk; entrees $18+; small dining room.
restaurants.blurb: Creative vegetarian dining downtown that omnivores book without being dragged — the menu reads globally and lands precisely.

restaurants.name: Sam Mazza's Farm Market
restaurants.blurb: A sprawling Colchester farm market — local produce, greenhouse plants, pick-your-own in season, and raspberry-filled cookies with a countywide reputation.

restaurants.name: Santiago's
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open, but closes 8pm Tue-Thu / 9pm Fri-Sat, closed Sun-Mon - no longer a late-night kitchen. https://santiagosvt.com/
restaurants.blurb: Open, but closes 8pm Tue-Thu / 9pm Fri-Sat, closed Sun-Mon - no longer a late-night kitchen. https://santiagosvt.com/

restaurants.name: Scout & Company
restaurants.attr_evidence: Small coffee + ice cream shop (seed Cozy/Solo-adjacent); quiet daytime spot; snacks rather than meals.
restaurants.blurb: Coffee and house-made ice cream under one small roof, with outposts on Pine Street, North Avenue, and in Winooski — the affogato is the reason both halves exist.

restaurants.name: Shacksbury Cider Tasting Room
restaurants.address: 75 Meigs Rd Dock A, Vergennes, VT 05491, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Vergennes tasting room — quiet cider flights at a counter; no real food; solo or a pair is the natural mode.
restaurants.blurb: Vergennes cidery working with wild and heritage apples — drier, stranger, and more wine-like than the sweet stuff the word "cider" usually promises.

restaurants.name: Shanty on the Shore
restaurants.attr_evidence: Lakeview deck (seed Both + Lakeside); sit-down seafood house works for conversation and groups; bar has TVs (local knowledge, low confidence); lobster roll ~$28, entrees mostly over $15.
restaurants.blurb: Burlington's waterfront seafood institution, with a deck over the water and a raw bar that doesn't pretend Vermont borders an ocean. The lobster roll is the standard order. Go for lunch when the deck isn't crowded.

restaurants.name: Shelburne Vineyard
restaurants.attr_evidence: Scenic winery tasting room + outdoor tables; quiet, conversation-first format; tastings $15-20 and no real meals; geared to pairs/groups more than solo.
restaurants.blurb: Cold-climate wines from grapes grown on the property — Marquette reds and La Crescent whites designed for Vermont winters. The grounds in September, when harvest is on, are the best argument for visiting in fall.

restaurants.name: Shy Guy Gelato
restaurants.address: 198 St Paul St, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.blurb: Small-batch gelato at St. Paul and Howard — Sicilian-style, dense and low-air, with vegan sorbets that aren't an afterthought. The flavor board changes; go with whatever's new.

restaurants.name: Sneakers Bistro
restaurants.attr_evidence: Winooski brunch institution with sidewalk tables; weekend rush is loud with waits; counter/solo brunch fine; most plates $10-16.
restaurants.blurb: Winooski's brunch institution, with a weekend wait to prove it — the maple 'magic bacon' has a following of its own.

restaurants.name: Snow Farm Vineyard
restaurants.address: 190 W Shore Rd, South Hero, VT 05486, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: South Hero vineyard lawn — scenic outdoor tastings and summer concert picnics suit groups; wine-tasting format, no meals.
restaurants.blurb: A South Hero vineyard you can reach by bike via the Island Line — cold-climate wines, lawn concerts on summer evenings, and the islands' unhurried pace built in.

restaurants.name: Sotto Enoteca
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open Wed-Sat 5-9pm + Sun; Yelp updated July 2026. https://www.sottoenoteca.co/
restaurants.blurb: Open Wed-Sat 5-9pm + Sun; Yelp updated July 2026. https://www.sottoenoteca.co/

restaurants.name: Southern Smoke
restaurants.address: 150 West Canal Street, unit 3, Winooski, VT, 05404

restaurants.name: Specs
cuisine: Cocktail bar (long-awaited DIY build-out by owner Nelis)
restaurants.address: Main St, Winooski, VT (exact number not found)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Grand opening Sunday Nov 16, 2025.
restaurants.blurb: Grand opening Sunday Nov 16, 2025.

restaurants.name: Speeder & Earl's
restaurants.attr_evidence: Longtime local coffee shop (seed Solo + Local Institution); easy solo, quiet; drinks/pastries, not meals.
restaurants.blurb: Pine Street's original coffee roaster, pouring since before the South End was an arts district — no fuss, deep roasts, longtime regulars.

restaurants.name: Splash at the Boathouse
restaurants.attr_evidence: Floating-dock bar, all-outdoor (roundups + seed); sunset crowd and music make it loud; bar seating easy solo; casual menu $10-18; TVs at the deck bar per visitor photos (low confidence).
restaurants.blurb: Casual dock food and drinks at the Community Boathouse, with direct lake views and the loosest dress code on the waterfront. Best for a beer while watching boat traffic clear the marina.

restaurants.name: Spot on the Dock
restaurants.attr_evidence: All-outdoor seasonal dock restaurant (seed Outdoor); busy sunny-day scene with music — not a quiet-talk spot; bar seating; seafood mains mostly $15+.
restaurants.blurb: Surf-shack dining on the water at King Street — fish tacos, frozen drinks, and boats pulling up close enough to critique. Sunset seating goes first.

restaurants.name: Stone Corral Brewery
restaurants.attr_evidence: Family-friendly Richmond taproom with lawn/outdoor seating (seed Both + Family tag); relaxed country pace; taproom food menu has cheap options (low confidence).
restaurants.blurb: Richmond's farmhouse taproom — the low-key stop on the I-89 corridor where locals actually drink while the tour buses head to Waterbury.

restaurants.name: Sweetwaters
cuisine: American bistro / Church Street patio institution, back after 4-year hiatus (chef Jessee Lawyer co-owner)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Also https://www.sevendaysvt.com/food-drink/foodnews/sweetwaters-to-reopen-on-burlingtons-church-street/
restaurants.blurb: Also https://www.sevendaysvt.com/food-drink/foodnews/sweetwaters-to-reopen-on-burlingtons-church-street/

restaurants.name: Switchback Brewing Co.
restaurants.attr_evidence: Indoor/outdoor taproom (seed), laid-back local crowd; taproom bar fine solo; limited food menu with cheap options.
restaurants.blurb: Vermont's most widely distributed beer, still made unfiltered and hazy the way it was in 1996. The taproom is an unfussy warehouse on Flynn Ave that hasn't chased trends and is better for it. Go on a weekday afternoon.

restaurants.name: Taco Gordo
restaurants.address: 208 N Winooski Ave, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Seed Both + back patio; lively bar-forward taqueria (mezcal list); counter/bar easy solo; tacos a la carte keep a meal under $15.
restaurants.blurb: Tacos and mezcal on North Winooski Avenue with tortillas made in-house — and Little Gordo, the creemee window bolted on for summer. Al pastor first, then argue about the rest.

restaurants.name: Thai in the Alley
cuisine: Thai restaurant (replaced BKK in the Alley, which closed fall 2025)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Opened Jan 30, 2026. Hours daily except Mon, noon-10pm.
restaurants.blurb: Opened Jan 30, 2026. Hours daily except Mon, noon-10pm.

restaurants.name: The Alchemist
restaurants.address: 100 Cottage Club Rd, Stowe, VT 05672, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Stowe visitor center: tastes + cans to go, outdoor area; daytime retail format — no meals, no TVs; groups fine to visit but it's not seated dining.
restaurants.blurb: The Stowe brewery behind Heady Topper, the double IPA that set off the haze craze nationally. Buy at the source — the visitor center pours tastes, and the cans never sit long.

restaurants.name: The Archives
restaurants.attr_evidence: Indoor arcade bar (thearchivesbar.com): 24 rotating taps, vintage games, snack-only menu (whipped feta etc.) — no real meals; screens are game cabinets, not sports; easy to post up solo, fun with groups, loud by design.
restaurants.blurb: A downtown bar with dozens of arcade games and pinball alongside craft beer and cocktails. The games actually work, which isn't a given for bars of this type.

restaurants.name: The Armory at the Hilton Garden Inn
The Friendly Toast
restaurants.address: 86 St Paul St, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Loud, kitschy all-day brunch chain; handles big tables well; bar seating; outdoor seating at Bank St location (med confidence); plates $13-18 straddle the $15 line.
restaurants.blurb: All-day breakfast in a retro, kitsch-crammed room near Church Street, with a menu long enough that indecisive tables graze for an hour. Bottomless coffee, generous plates.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: Chain runs weekday afternoon drink/app specials; verify Burlington location's current schedule

restaurants.name: The Grey Jay
restaurants.attr_evidence: Seven Days: packed brunch room with 20-25 min waits at noon — lively, not quiet; counter/bar seating works solo; brunch dishes mostly at/under $15 (low confidence).
restaurants.blurb: A polished downtown room doing modern American plates and a serious cocktail list — brunch and dinner that feel like an occasion, a block off Church Street.

restaurants.name: The Harborvale
cuisine: 'Timeless American' restaurant, cafe, and late-night bar in the reborn hotel (ex-Hotel Vermont team, chef Doug Paine); open to the public 7 days
restaurants.attr_evidence: Hotel officially opened July 7, 2026. Replaced Bleu Northeast Kitchen. Also covered: https://www.sevendaysvt.com/food-drink/foodnews/the-harborvale-hotel-and-restaurant-to-launch-in-burlington/
restaurants.blurb: Hotel officially opened July 7, 2026. Replaced Bleu Northeast Kitchen. Also covered: https://www.sevendaysvt.com/food-drink/foodnews/the-harborvale-hotel-and-restaurant-to-launch-in-burlington/

restaurants.name: The Kitchen Table Market & Cafe
cuisine: Market and cafe (Kitchen Table restaurant group from Richmond); breakfast sandwiches, paninis
restaurants.attr_evidence: Opened Nov 28, 2025.
restaurants.blurb: Opened Nov 28, 2025.

restaurants.name: The Old Post
restaurants.address: 733 Queen City Park Rd, South Burlington, VT, 05403

restaurants.name: The Other Place (OP)
restaurants.address: 4 N Winooski Ave #1, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Yelp/ChalkySticks: sports-bar/pool-hall dive, 'place to watch football', pool table, darts, $3 PBR mini-pints; classic solo barstool spot; free popcorn but no real meals.
restaurants.blurb: The OP is a classic dive in the best sense — cheap pitchers, pool, zero pretension, and a crowd that spans every Burlington demographic by 10 PM.

restaurants.name: The Parkway Diner
restaurants.address: 1696 Williston Rd, South Burlington, VT 05403, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Vintage railcar diner in South Burlington — counter stools ideal solo; railcar footprint can't seat 8 together; breakfast/lunch under $15.
restaurants.blurb: A genuine 1950s stainless-steel diner car on Williston Road, restored and cooking — the real article, chrome and all.

restaurants.name: The Pour House
restaurants.address: 1930 Williston Rd STE 5, South Burlington, VT, 05403

restaurants.name: The Skinny Pancake
restaurants.attr_evidence: Covered waterfront patio cited in Seven Days/skinnypancake outdoor-dining roundups; casual counter-order creperie, crepes $8-14; easy solo, fine for groups.
restaurants.blurb: Sweet and savory crepes on the waterfront, with patio tables that fill up by noon in summer. The maple-apple crepe in fall is hard to argue with. Local ingredients throughout.

restaurants.name: The Wise Fool
restaurants.attr_evidence: Cozy, quirky ONE spot (seed tags); small room — intimate conversation yes, party of 8 no; Monday shawarma keeps a meal near/under $15 (low confidence on regular menu).
restaurants.blurb: A small Old North End kitchen with a devoted following — Monday shawarma night has its own word-of-mouth economy. Off the tourist path entirely, which is the point.

restaurants.name: Three Needs Taproom & Brewery
restaurants.attr_evidence: Divey taproom institution; cheap pints, easy to drink solo; no real food menu so under_15 is false as a meal; loud in the evening.
restaurants.blurb: A downtown brewpub-dive hybrid where catching Duff Hour — the famously cheap early-evening pint window — is a local rite of passage. Slices available late.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: 'Duff Hour' — famously cheap house pints daily (seed cost note); exact hour varies, verify

restaurants.name: Tikka House Indian Cuisine
cuisine: Indian-Nepali (biryani, tandoori kebabs, kati rolls); took over former Grazers space
restaurants.attr_evidence: Opened March 22, 2025.
restaurants.blurb: Opened March 22, 2025.

restaurants.name: Top of the Block Sandwich Shop
Trapp Family Lodge & Bierhall
restaurants.address: 700 Trapp Hill Rd, Stowe, VT 05672, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Stowe bierhall with mountain-view deck; big echoey hall with communal tables — built for groups, hard for quiet talk; bar fine solo; Austrian mains mostly $16-24.
restaurants.blurb: Yes, the Sound of Music family — the von Trapps settled in Stowe, and their lodge now brews Austrian-style lagers served in a timber bierhall with mountain views.

restaurants.name: Uncommon Coffee
cuisine: Coffee roaster/cafe, successor to Uncommon Grounds
restaurants.attr_evidence: The Burlington shop (Uncommon Grounds, 42 Church St) closed Dec 2019; Uncommon Coffee operates in Essex only - do not list as Burlington. https://uncommonvt.com/pages/about
restaurants.blurb: The Burlington shop (Uncommon Grounds, 42 Church St) closed Dec 2019; Uncommon Coffee operates in Essex only - do not list as Burlington. https://uncommonvt.com/pages/about

restaurants.name: Upper Pass Beer Co.
cuisine: Brewery taproom with food-truck pop-ups; took over the former Simple Roots Brewing space
restaurants.address: 1127 North Ave (Ethan Allen Shopping Center), Burlington, VT 05408
restaurants.attr_evidence: Opening day May 6, 2026. Moved from Tunbridge.
restaurants.blurb: Opening day May 6, 2026. Moved from Tunbridge.

restaurants.name: Venetian Soda Lounge
restaurants.attr_evidence: Cozy, quirky cocktail/soda lounge (seed tags); lounge format favors conversation and bar seats; craft-cocktail pricing, snack-level food.
restaurants.blurb: A cocktail lounge inside the Soda Plant, named for the ginger ale once bottled in the building — the drinks nod to the soda history without turning it into a theme park.

restaurants.name: Vermont Pub & Brewery
restaurants.attr_evidence: Classic brewpub with seasonal covered outdoor seating on College St; long bar good solo, TVs at the bar; big dining room handles groups; pub fare includes sub-$15 plates. TV/patio from local knowledge, not confirmed in roundups.
restaurants.blurb: Vermont's original brewpub, opened in 1988 by Greg Noonan, whose textbook on lager brewing is still referenced by professional brewers. The booths in back are older than most of the state's craft breweries.

restaurants.name: Vietnamese Pho Ly
cuisine: Vietnamese (pho, bun bowls, banh mi); family-owned, in former Fish & Chip space
restaurants.attr_evidence: Opened April 21, 2025.
restaurants.blurb: Opened April 21, 2025.

restaurants.name: Village Scoop
restaurants.blurb: Colchester's creemee-and-scoop window — the after-beach, after-game, after-anything stop for the whole north end of the county.

restaurants.name: Vivid Coffee
cuisine: Coffee roaster/cafe (Vivid Coffee Roasters, started in Winooski 2015)
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open daily; Yelp updated July 2026. https://vividcoffee.com/visit
restaurants.blurb: Open daily; Yelp updated July 2026. https://vividcoffee.com/visit

restaurants.name: Wallflower Collective
cuisine: Cozy cocktail bar (couches, fireplace, pool tables); no kitchen - patrons order Folino's pizza from next door
restaurants.attr_evidence: Open ~4pm-2am most nights; women/queer/trans-welcoming; 5% profits to charity.
restaurants.blurb: Open ~4pm-2am most nights; women/queer/trans-welcoming; 5% profits to charity.

restaurants.name: Waterworks Food + Drink
restaurants.address: 20 Winooski Falls Wy, Winooski, VT 05404, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Big deck over the Winooski River in the Champlain Mill; spacious room handles conversation and large parties (dedicated private-events program); sizable bar; entrees mostly $18+.
restaurants.blurb: Built into the old Champlain Mill with windows directly over the Winooski falls — ask for a window table and time it for spring melt, when the river is deafening and the glass barely holds the drama back.
restaurants.happy_hour_note: Happy hour specials confirmed on waterworksvt.com promos page; kids eat free Sun-Thu; exact HH times unverified

restaurants.name: Weird Window Brewing
restaurants.address: 82 Ethan Allen Dr suite a, South Burlington, VT, 05403

restaurants.name: Willow's Bagels
restaurants.address: 71 S Union St, Burlington, VT 05401, USA
restaurants.attr_evidence: Small ONE bagel shop; cheap breakfast sandwiches; counter format easy solo.
restaurants.blurb: The Old North End's bagel stop — hand-rolled, boiled, and gone by late morning on weekends. Get there before the neighborhood does.

restaurants.name: Zabby & Elf's Stone Soup
restaurants.attr_evidence: Mellow pay-by-weight buffet + bakery (seed Solo tag); window seats and small tables perfect solo; small room for big groups.
restaurants.blurb: Homemade vegetarian-leaning food served cafeteria-style on College Street — the hot bar changes daily and the bread is baked in-house. Half of downtown's vegetarians eat here weekly.

restaurants.name: Zero Gravity Craft Brewery
restaurants.attr_evidence: Beer hall + big beer garden on Pine St (Yelp/press); GoodRec lists it as a spot locals watch soccer; long-table beer hall is loud but group-friendly; pub menu has items under $15.
restaurants.blurb: The Conehead IPA is what most people come for; the Clean State Pilsner is what makes regulars of them. Pine Street taproom is quieter than Foam and better suited to a long conversation.
